title: Communication path management
abstract: A mobile device connects to an anchor point and may determine a plurality of internet protocol (IP) addresses, with each of the plurality of IP addresses being associated with a communication path. The mobile device stores each of the plurality of IP addresses in relation to one of a plurality of interface identifiers. Each of the plurality of interface identifiers may correspond to a physical communication interface such as a WiFi interface or cellular interface. The mobile device may use the stored data to select an IP address corresponding to a communication path based at least upon the interface identifier stored in relation to the one of the plurality of IP addresses. The mobile device may also store an SCS type for each IP address and may use the SCS types associated with IP addresses to determine whether a particular IP address should be used to create a communication path.
claims:
Claims CLAI S What is Claimed: 1 , A method for managing communication paths, comprising: receiving, by a WTRU, a piuraiity of internet protocoi (IP) addresses, each IP address received from one of a plurality of access nodes with which the WTRU establishes a connection; storing, by the WTRU, the plurality of IP addresses, each IP address stored in relation to one of a piuraiity of interface identifiers, each of the piuraiity of interface identifiers associated with a communication interface; communicating, by the WTRU, via a first communication path associated with a first of the plurality of IP addresses and a first interface identifier stored in relation to the first of the plurality of IP addresses; determining, by the WTRU, a second communication path should be identified; selecting, by the WTRU, for the second communication path, a second of the plurality of IP addresses based at least upon the first interface identifier and a second Interface identifier stored in relation to the second of the plurality of IP addresses, 2, The method of claim 1 , wherein receiving a plurality of IP addresses comprises receiving IP addresses from anchor nodes associated with one or more of the following: a WiFi network; a 3GPP network; and a Bluetooth network. 3, The method of claim 1 , wherein storing the piuraiity of IP addresses comprises storing the plurality of IP addresses in a communication protocol stack, the communication protocol stack identifying tor each of the plurality of IP addresses one of the plurality of interface identifiers. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein storing the plurality of IP addresses in a communication protocol stack comprises storing the plurality of IP addresses In a Multi-path TCP ( PTCP) stack, 5, The method of claim 3, wherein storing the plurality of IP addresses in a communication protocol stack comprises storing the piuraiity of IP addresses in a Stream Control Transmission Protocol (SCTP) stack. 6, The method of claim 1 , wherein one or more of the Interface identifiers comprises a group identifier, 7, The method of claim 6, wherein the group identifier corresponds to a plurality of logical interfaces that map to a physical interface. 8, The method of claim 1 , wherein determining a second communication path should be identified comprises determining a second communication path is needed to provide additional bandwidth; and wherein selecting for the second communication path, a second of the plurality of IP addresses comprises selecting the second of the piuraiity of IP addresses based at ieast upon the second interface identifier stored in relation to the second of the piuraiity of IP addresses being different than the first interface identifier associated with the first IP address. 9, The method of claim 8, further comprising: communicating data via the first communication path and the second communication path. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: communicating data via the second communication path if the first communication path becomes inoperable, wherein determining a second communication path should be identified comprises determining a second communication path is needed to provide backup bandwidth; and wherein selecting for the second communication path, a second of the plurality of IP addresses comprises selecting the second of the plurality of IP addresses based at least upon the second interface identifier stored in relation to the second of the plurality of IP addresses being different than the first interface identifier associated with the first IP address. The method of claim 1 , further comprising: storing, by the WTRU, for each of the plurality of IP addresses, state information indicating whether a direct network connection exists; wherein determining a second communication path should be identified comprises determining a second communication path with a short data path should be identified, and wherein selecting for the second communication path , a second of the plurality of IP addresses comprises selecting the second of the plurality of IP addresses based at least upon the second of the plurality of IP addresses having stored therewith state Information indicating a direct network connection exists, and the second interface Identifier stored in relation to the second of the plurality of IP addresses being the same as the first interface Identifier associated with the first IP address, The method of claim 1 1 , further comprising: updating for the first of the plurality of IP addresses state information to indicate an Indirect network connection exists. 13. A method of managing communication paths, comprising: determining, by a WTRU, a first internet protocol (IP) address for use with a communication session; identifying, by the WTRU, a first SCS type associated with the first IP address; storing, by the WTRU, the first IP address in association with the identified first SCS type; communicating, by the WTRU, the first SCS type to a second WTRU; determining, by the WTRU, a second IP address in response to the WTRU connecting to a new network access point; identifying, by the WTRU, a second SCS type associated with the second IP address, determining, by the WTRU, to employ the second IP address to communicate data based at least upon the first SCS type and the second SCS type. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: identifying, by the WTRU, a first compatibility group associated with the first IP address; storing, by the WTRU, the first compatibility group in association with the first IP address; identifying, by the WTRU, a second compatibility group associated with the second IP address; storing, by the WTRU, the second compatibility group associated with the second IP address; determining, by the WTRU, not to employ the second IP address to communicate data based at least upon the second compatibility group not corresponding to the first compatibility group. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein determining to employ the second IP address to communicate data based at least upon the first SCS type and the second SCS type comprises: 

 determining to employ the second !P address to communicate data based at ieast upon the first SCS type having a value of at least one of Fixed and Session Lasting, and the second SCS type having a value of Non-Persistent. The method of claim 13. wherein determining to employ the second IP address to communicate data based at Ieast upon the first SCS type and the second SCS type comprises: determining to employ the second IP address as a backup to the first IP address based at Ieast upon the first SCS type having a value of Non-Persistent and the second SCS type having a value o Non-Persistent. 17, The method of claim 13, wherein determining employ the second IP address to communicate data based at least upon the first SCS type and the second SCS type comprises: determining to employ the second IP address as a backup in replacement of the first IP address based at Ieast upon the first SCS type having a value of Graceful Replacement and the second SCS type having a value of Graceful Replacement. 18. The method of claim 13, further comprising: storing, by the WTRU, the second IP address in association with the second SCS type: and communicating, by the WTRU, the second SCS type to the second WTRU. 19. The method of claim 18, 

 wherein storing the first IP address in association with the first SCS type and storing the second IP address in association with the second SCS type comprises storing the first IP address, first SCS type, second IP address, and second SCS type in a communication protocol stack, A method of managing communication paths, comprising: receiving, from a VVTRU, a first internet protocol (IP) address for use with a communication session; receiving, from the WTRU, a first SCS type associated with the first IP address; storing, by the WTRU, the first IP address in association with the identified first SCS type; receiving, from the WTRU, a second IP address; receiving, from the WTRU, a second SCS type associated with the second IP address, determining to employ the second IP address to communicate data based at least upon the first SCS type and the second SCS type.
